canonical_name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (Ayushman Bharat)

income_thresholds.description: Annual household income must be below Rs 2.5 lakh as per SECC 2011 deprivation criteria

exclusion_rules.description: Families where any member has employer-provided or company-sponsored health insurance (including group mediclaim, ESIS coverage, or any coverage >=Rs 10,000/year) are excluded
Government employees and their families are excluded
Any family member who has filed an Income Tax Return in any of the past 3 assessment years disqualifies the entire household, even if zero tax was paid
Families owning any motorized two-wheeler, three-wheeler, four-wheeler, or mechanized fishing boat at any time in the past 24 months are excluded
Families owning mechanized three- or four-wheeler agricultural equipment are excluded
Families with Kisan Credit Card with sanctioned limit (not utilized amount) above Rs 50,000 are excluded
Families owning more than 2.5 acres of irrigated land with at least one irrigation equipment are excluded; threshold may vary by state (check BIS portal for state-specific rules)

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as defined in SECC 2011 database; no cap on family size. Coverage is Rs 5 lakh floater across all family members listed in the Ayushman card. Any family member can use any amount up to Rs 5 lakh total per year. Coverage resets each year on the anniversary of card issuance.

covered_procedures: General Surgery (hernia, appendectomy, cholecystectomy, etc.)
Cardiology & Cardiothoracic (bypass, valve replacement, angioplasty, pacemaker)
Orthopedics (knee/hip replacement, fracture fixation, spine surgery)
Oncology (chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surgical oncology for all cancers)
Neurosurgery (brain tumour, aneurysm clipping, VP shunt)
Urology (kidney stone, prostatectomy, bladder surgery)
Nephrology (dialysis, renal transplant)
ENT / Otolaryngology (cochlear implant, tympanoplasty, tonsillectomy)
Gastroenterology (endoscopy, liver biopsy, ERCP)
Pulmonology (TB treatment, lung surgery, ventilator support)
Emergency & Trauma (polytrauma, acute care, snake/animal bite)
Pediatrics & Neonatal (NICU, pediatric surgery, congenital defects)
Obstetrics & Gynecology (C-section, hysterectomy, high-risk pregnancy)
Ophthalmology (cataract, glaucoma, retinal surgery, cornea transplant)
Dermatology (skin grafting, burn treatment)
Psychiatry & Mental Health (acute psychiatric hospitalization)
Vascular Surgery (varicose veins, AV fistula for dialysis)
Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery (post-burn reconstruction, cleft lip/palate)
Burns & Specialized ICU
Dental & Maxillofacial (jaw surgery, trauma, oral cancer)
Rheumatology (joint-related hospitalizations)
Infectious Diseases (TB, HIV/AIDS, Hepatitis treatment during hospitalization)
Pediatric Surgery (congenital anomalies, neonatal surgery)
Day-care procedures (no overnight stay - cataract, dialysis, chemotherapy sessions)
Pre-hospitalization diagnostics (up to 3 days before admission)
Post-hospitalization follow-up (up to 15 days after discharge including medicines)
Transport allowance (Rs 1,000 per hospitalization for travel to empanelled hospital)

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for e-KYC verification at empanelled hospital or CSC

required_documents.name: Ration Card (NFSA)
required_documents.notes: NFSA ration card holders are auto-included. If not available, SECC inclusion letter is checked.

required_documents.name: SECC 2011 Inclusion Letter
required_documents.notes: Letter confirming family is listed in SECC 2011 deprivation database under D1-D7 categories

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: Linked to Aadhaar for OTP-based verification. Family member's number acceptable.

required_documents.name: Family Photo
required_documents.notes: Required for new Ayushman card generation at CSC or hospital

required_documents.name: Income Certificate
required_documents.notes: Alternative proof when SECC letter unavailable; issued by Tehsildar/SDM

required_documents.name: PAN Card or Form 60
required_documents.notes: Used for income tax payer verification (exclusion rule PMJAY-EX-003 check)

enrollment_channels: Common Service Centre (CSC / Jan Seva Kendra)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Check eligibility on pmjay.gov.in or mera.pmjay.gov.in or call 14555 with Aadhaar/ration card number
Step 2: Visit nearest CSC (Jan Seva Kendra) or empanelled hospital with Aadhaar card and ration card
Step 3: Complete e-KYC verification (Aadhaar biometric fingerprint or OTP on linked mobile)
Step 4: Operator verifies SECC 2011 inclusion status in the BIS (Beneficiary Information System) portal
Step 5: If SECC-verified, family photograph taken and all member details entered
Step 6: Ayushman card (Golden Card) generated with unique ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) ID
Step 7: Card printed on the spot (digital copy also available on Ayushman App)
Step 8: Card is valid immediately across 27,000+ empanelled hospitals in all states (except WB and DL)
Step 9: For treatment, present Ayushman Card + Aadhaar at any empanelled hospital for 100% cashless admission
Step 10: No premium, no copayment, no deductible — treatment is completely free for the beneficiary
description_for_embedding: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY / Ayushman Bharat) is India's flagship central government health insurance providing Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. Covers 55 crore beneficiaries from economically vulnerable families identified through SECC 2011 deprivation criteria (D1-D7). Provides 1,393 medical packages across 26 specialties including surgery, cardiology, oncology, orthopedics, dialysis, and neonatal care at 27,000+ empanelled public and private hospitals. Completely free — no premium, copayment, or deductible. Includes 3-day pre-hospitalization and 15-day post-hospitalization coverage. Cashless and portable across all states except West Bengal and Delhi (which have their own schemes). Enrollment via CSC, hospital, app, or 14555 helpline. Golden Card/Ayushman Card issued on the spot with Aadhaar e-KYC. Excludes: government employees, income tax payers, employer-insured families, and asset-owning households per SECC criteria.
